definition:
γρηγορέω, -ῶ, = Attic ἐργήγορα, pf. of ἐγείρω, which see, 
 [in LXX (later bks. only) chiefly for שׁקד ;] 
   1. to be awake; metaphorically, of being alive, 1Th.5:10. 
   2. to watch: Mat.24:43 26:38, 40 Mrk.13:34 14:34, 37 Luk.12:37, 39; metaphorically, Mat.24:42 25:13 26:41, Mrk.13:35, 37 14:38, Act.20:31, 1Co.16:13, 1Th.5:6, 1Pe.5:8, Rev.3:2, 3 16:15; before ἐν, Col.4:2. (Cf. δια-γρηγορέω).†
 SYN.: see: ἀγρθπνέω. (AS)
